---
pagetitle: NKDE Analysis | Capstone
title: NKDE Analysis using spNetwork package
---

![](../../Images/4.png){width="500"}

# Getting Started

The code chunk below uses `p_load()` function of pacman package to check if the required packages have been installed on the computer. If they are, the packages will be launched.

The packages used are:

-   sf package is used for importing, managing, and processing geospatial data.
-   tidyverse package for aspatial data wrangling.
-   spatstat package to perform 1st-order spatial point patterns analysis and derive kernel density estimation (KDE) layer.
-   tmap package is used to visualize geospatial data on maps.

```{r}
pacman::p_load(sf, tidyverse, spatstat, lubridate, tmap, spNetwork, RColorBrewer)
```

In the code chunk below, the [cleaned data files](DataPrep.qmd) are loaded into R.

```{r}
# Load base map
mpsz <- st_read(dsn = "../data/geospatial", 
               layer = "MPSZ-2019") %>%
  st_transform(crs = 3414) %>%
  filter(!PLN_AREA_N %in% c("WESTERN ISLANDS", "NORTH-EASTERN ISLANDS"))

# Load rds file
pickup_sf <- st_as_sf(read_rds("../data/rds/pickup_sf.rds"))
dropoff_sf <- st_as_sf(read_rds("../data/rds/dropoff_sf.rds"))
```

The code chunk below uses `st_read()` function of sf package to import the OpenStreetMap \> Asia \> Malaysia, Singapore and Brunei shapefile extract from the [GeoFabrik website](https://download.geofabrik.de/). As this set of geospatial data is in WGS-84 coordinate system, `st_transform()` function is used to convert it to the svy21 projected coordinate system (`crs = 3414`) that Singapore uses.

```{r}
#| eval: false
roads <- st_read(dsn = "../data/geospatial", 
               layer = "gis_osm_roads_free_1") %>%
  st_transform(crs = 3414) %>%
  st_sf()

roads_sg <- st_cast(mpsz %>% 
                      filter(PLN_AREA_N == "EAST REGION") %>%
                      summarize(), "POLYGON") %>%
  st_intersection(roads)

str(roads_sg)
```

A check is performed to ensure that the geometry of all observations are linestrings. Since some are multilinestrings, they are cast into multipoints then merged back to form a single linestring. 

```{r}
roads_sg_check <- roads_sg %>%
  mutate(multi = st_geometry_type(geometry)) %>%
  filter(multi != "LINESTRING")

# Cast multilinestrings into linestrings
roads_sg_check_merged <- roads_sg_check %>%
  st_cast('MULTIPOINT') %>%
  st_cast('LINESTRING')

# Merge back with linestrings
roads_sg_lines <- roads_sg %>%
  mutate(multi = st_geometry_type(geometry)) %>%
  filter(multi == "LINESTRING") %>%
  rbind(roads_sg_check_merged)

roads_sf <- st_cast(roads_sg_lines, "LINESTRING")

str(roads_sf)
```

# Visualising the data

```{r}
str(sg_road)
```

```{r}
# Calculate the length of each linestring geometry
lengths <- st_length(sg_road)
print(min(lengths))
```

```{r}
#| eval: false
# Calculate lixels
lixels <- lixelize_lines(sg_road, 500, mindist = 100)
samples <- lines_center(lixels)

# Apply NKDE
densities <- nkde(sg_road, 
                  events = pickup_sf,
                  w = rep(1,nrow(pickup_sf)),
                  samples = samples,
                  kernel_name = "quartic",
                  bw = 500, div= "bw", 
                  method = "discontinuous", digits = 1, tol = 1,
                  grid_shape = c(2,2), max_depth = 8,
                  agg = 10, #we aggregate events within a 5m radius (faster calculation)
                  sparse = TRUE,
                  verbose = FALSE)
```

```{r}
#| eval: false
# rescaling to help the mapping
samples$density <- samples$density*1000

samples2 <- samples[order(samples$density),]

colorRamp <- brewer.pal(n = 7, name = "Spectral")
colorRamp <- rev(colorRamp)

title <- paste0("bike accident density by kilometres in 2016,",
                "\nwithin a radius of 300 metres")

tm_shape(sg_road) + 
  tm_lines("black") + 
  tm_shape(samples2) + 
  tm_dots("density", style = "kmeans", palette = colorRamp, n = 7, size = 0.1) + 
  tm_layout(legend.outside = TRUE, 
            main.title = title , main.title.size = 1)
```
